question: Generate an SSH key pair for GitHub authentication using the Ed25519 algorithm, stored in ~/.ssh/github_id_rsa with no passphrase.
validation_script: #!/bin/bash\nif [ -f ~/.ssh/github_id_rsa ] && [ -f ~/.ssh/github_id_rsa.pub ] && grep -q '^ssh-ed25519' ~/.ssh/github_id_rsa.pub; then exit 0; else exit 1; fi

question: Display the contents of your GitHub public key (~/.ssh/github_id_rsa.pub) to verify it was created correctly.
Generate a second SSH key pair for managing Rocky Linux nodes, stored as ~/.ssh/rocky_id_rsa with Ed25519 algorithm and no passphrase.
validation_script: #!/bin/bash\nif [ -f ~/.ssh/rocky_id_rsa ] && [ -f ~/.ssh/rocky_id_rsa.pub ] && grep -q '^ssh-ed25519' ~/.ssh/rocky_id_rsa.pub; then exit 0; else exit 1; fi

question: Copy the Rocky public key to the managed node at 172.16.120.11 using ssh-copy-id (you will be prompted for the user's password).
validation_script: #!/bin/bash\nssh -o BatchMode=yes -o ConnectTimeout=3 student@172.16.120.11 true 2>/dev/null && exit 0 || exit 1

question: Verify that you can log in to the Rocky node without a password by running a command that prints the hostname.
validation_script: #!/bin/bash\noutput=$(ssh -o BatchMode=yes -o ConnectTimeout=3 student@172.16.120.11 hostname 2>/dev/null) && [[ "$output" == "rocky9" ]] && exit 0 || exit 1
